title: OAM-mode sorting with a wavefront twister
authors: Suman Karan, Swati Chaudhary, Harshwardhan Wanare, Anand Kumar Jha
abstract: We propose an OAM sorter based on a novel optical element that we refer to as a wavefront twister. It is a generalization of the conventional wavefront rotators such as the Dove prism. However, unlike a Dove prism, which simply rotates a wavefront, the rotation generated by a wavefront twister varies linearly with radial position, resulting in the twisting of the wavefront. We demonstrate that the wavefront twister, followed by a lens, maps each OAM mode to an annulus of distinct radius at the back focal plane of the lens with negligible inter-modal overlap and preserves the circular symmetry. Thus, the proposed wavefront twister offers a scalable scheme for high-dimensional OAM mode sorting, with important consequences for the practical realization of OAM-based applications.
summary: The paper introduces a 'wavefront twister' optical element that sorts OAM modes into concentric rings of different radii. By increasing the twisting strength, the researchers demonstrate that inter-mode crosstalk can be almost entirely eliminated, offering a scalable path for high-dimensional OAM-based applications.
main_problem: The challenge of efficiently and unambiguously sorting high-dimensional Orbital Angular Momentum (OAM) modes while minimizing inter-modal crosstalk and diffraction loss.
main_result: The proposed 'wavefront twister' maps OAM modes to distinct annular rings at the focal plane, where increasing the twisting strength can reduce crosstalk from 15.45% to 0.05%.
method: The authors use the Fresnel-Kirchhoff diffraction integral and paraxial approximation to calculate electric field intensity, employing power series expansions and hypergeometric functions for analytical evaluation.
model_or_system: An optical system consisting of a novel 'wavefront twister' element—which applies a radially dependent rotation theta(rho) = a*rho—followed by a convex lens, acting on Laguerre-Gaussian (LG) beams.
key_observables: OAM index (l), mean radial position (mu), radial spread (sigma), and inter-modal crosstalk percentage.
important_parameters: Twisting strength (a/w0), OAM topological charge (l), and beam waist (w0).
main_assumptions: The study assumes the paraxial approximation for LG beams and focuses on the p=0 radial mode.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the conceptual design of the twister vs. a standard rotator; Fig 2 and 3 show 2D/1D intensity profiles at different twisting strengths; Fig 4 plots radial position and spread vs. l; Fig 5 shows crosstalk vs. twisting strength.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the wavefront twister concept, provides the mathematical framework for the coordinate transformation and diffraction, presents numerical simulations of mode separation and crosstalk, and discusses scalability and limitations.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant to quantum optics and photonic quantum information, as it proposes a scalable method for managing high-dimensional OAM states, which are essential for high-capacity quantum communication and encoding.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Conceptual illustration of the proposed wavefront twister and the OAM sorting scheme. (a) A conventional wavefront rotator such as a Dove prism, oriented at angle θ0 rotates an input wavefront by angle 2θ0. (b) The proposed wavefront twister rotates an incoming wavefront by an angle θ(ρ) = aρ that varies linearly with radial location ρ. Con- sequently, the wavefront is not simply rotated but twisted. (c) The wavefront twister maps the point (ρ, ϕ) in the inci- dent wavefront to (ρ, ϕ + aρ). (d) The proposed OAM sorter scheme consisting of the wavefront twister followed by a lens of focal length f maps the OAM modes l1 and l2 to annuli of distinct radii in the focal plane, leading to...

FIG. 2. (a) Numerically computed two-dimensional intensity |E(r, θ, f = 200 mm)|2 for OAM mode index l ranging from 1 to 20 at a/w0 = 1π and w0 = 0.1 cm. (b) Numerically computer one-dimensional intensity profiles |E(r, θ = 0, f = 200 mm)|2 as a function of r for OAM mode index l ranging from 1 to 20 at a/w0 = 1π and w0 = 0.1 cm.

FIG. 3. (a) Numerically computed two-dimensional intensity |E(r, θ, f = 200 mm)|2 for OAM mode index l ranging from 1 to 20 at a/w0 = 3π and w0 = 0.1 cm. (b) Numerically computer one-dimensional intensity profiles |E(r, θ = 0, f = 200 mm)|2 as a function of r for OAM mode index l ranging from 1 to 20 at a/w0 = 3π and w0 = 0.1 cm.
FIG. 4. (a) Plot of the mean radial position µ of the ring formed by each sorted OAM mode as a function of the OAM index l (b) Radial spread σ of each ring as a function of l.
Fig. 2 (a) shows the numerically computed two- dimensional intensity profiles |E(r, θ, f = 200 mm)|2

FIG. 5. Plot of consecutive mode crosstalk as a function of a/w0. The overlap percentage decreases monotonically with increasing a/w0. The highlighted points correspond to crosstalk percentages for a/w0 = 1π and 3π.
